features.code_shell: Shell or command-line script code (bash, sh, zsh, PowerShell)
features.code_other: Source code in other languages (C/C++, Go, Ruby, Rust, etc.)
features.data_xml: XML data records (not generic HTML pages)
features.markup_xml: Non-HTML XML markup (custom tags, XML schemas, feeds)
features.markdown_syntax: Markdown inline formatting (bold, italics, backticks, links)
features.encoding_other: Other opaque encodings or ciphertext-like blobs
features.table: Rows and columns layout (ASCII tables, Markdown tables, HTML tables)
features.lists: Bulleted or numbered lists where each item is separate line
features.headers: Section or subsection titles (document or Markdown headings) Must be accompianied with formatting.
features.steps_or_phases: Ordered sequential instructions (Step 1, Step 2, Phase A, etc.)
features.email_elements: Email-specific structure (subject line, to/from, greeting, signature)
features.multi_turn_chat: Back-and-forth conversation with multiple turns between speakers
features.quantitative_data: Non-financial numeric information (counts, metrics, percentages, statistics, measurements). Tag if numbers are prominent even if not dominant.
features.financial_figures: Monetary values and currency amounts (prices, budgets, P&L lines, revenue)
features.dates_times: Explicit dates, times, or time ranges (calendar dates, timestamps, quarters, deadlines)
features.formulas: Mathematical or symbolic equations and expressions
features.url: URLs or web addresses (http://, https://, www.)
features.file_paths: File system paths or directory locations (/, \, C:\, /usr/bin)
features.error_messages: System or application error output including error codes, stack traces, exception messages. Tag even if embedded in code or logs.
features.credentials: Passwords, tokens, API keys, or other authentication secrets visible in text
features.plain_text: Natural language prose that is not primarily any structured format above

core_tagging: Tag MULTIPLE features when applicable (e.g., code_python + error_messages + quantitative_data)
DO NOT limit to only one feature - return ALL that apply
When in doubt, err on the side of inclusion

do_tag_code: Code with statements: 'def function(): return x'
Syntax elements: braces, semicolons, keywords, function definitions
do_not_tag_code: Extensions: 'file.java' or 'script.py' mentioned in text (not code itself)
The word 'java' or 'python' in a path (does NOT mean code is present)
File paths that mention language: '/src/main/java/com/example/...' (NOT code)
Just the word 'java' or 'python' in natural language
code_detection.rule: Only tag code features when you see actual programming syntax

other_rules: Tag 'steps' ONLY if enumerated steps exist in text
